title: Andreessen Horowitz Is Playing Politics Like No Other
summary: “If you think there’s a lot of money in politics now,” Marc Andreessen said in 2000, “you haven’t seen anything yet.” His firm is now the biggest known spender on this campaign cycle.

title: Silicon Valley’s A.I. Lobbying Blitz Reaches a Fever Pitch
summary: OpenAI and Anthropic are opening offices in Washington, hiring lobbyists and spending more than ever to win over federal lawmakers.

title: Anduril Raises $5 Billion in Funding and Is Valued at $61 Billion
summary: The start-up, which makes A.I.-backed weapons, was valued at $61 billion in the financing round, double what it was a year ago.

title: Notable Researchers Join $4 Billion Effort to Build Self-Improving A.I.
summary: Recursive Superintelligence, founded by former Google, Meta and OpenAI researchers, is part of a growing effort to automate the creation of artificial intelligence.

title: Perfect Timing, Big Payouts: Insider Trading Red Flags Emerge on Polymarket
summary: Dozens of long-shot bets on Polymarket, from the war with Iran to the cryptocurrency market, have defied the odds, according to a New York Times examination.

title: Fuel Prices Drive Sales of E.V.s, Just Not in the U.S.
summary: Electric vehicle sales have soared in Europe and much of the rest of the world, but Americans are still hesitant.

title: Tired of Hacked Passwords? Help Is on the Way.
summary: Apps from Apple, Google and others can assist in making your online accounts more secure, even as new ways of logging in continue to take off.

title: Nvidia C.E.O. Jensen Huang Hitches Ride With Trump to China After Last-Minute Invite
summary: Jensen Huang boarded Air Force One in Alaska, joining a delegation of more than a dozen business leaders accompanying President Trump on his trip to Beijing.

title: Anthropic in Talks to Raise Funding at a $950 Billion Valuation
summary: The start-up, which recently released a powerful A.I. model called Mythos and is separately battling with the Pentagon, was previously valued at $380 billion.

title: Musk Lawyer’s Question for Sam Altman on the Stand: Are You Trustworthy?
summary: Mr. Altman, the C.E.O. of OpenAI, said on Tuesday that he worried Elon Musk wanted control of the A.I. lab.

title: Sam Altman has been targeted in a backlash against A.I.
This is Sam Altman’s first time testifying in court.
summary: The chief executive

title: Is Anthropic’s Claude Mythos Really a Cybersecurity Risk?
summary: Anthropic said that Claude Mythos was too dangerous to release to the public. That claim has reopened an old debate over cybersecurity.

title: A.I. and Humans Battle It Out in a Cybersecurity Showdown
summary: Experts and college students used A.I. agents to try to break into and defend computer networks in a national competition. The agents did all right on their own, too.

title: Here’s the latest.
EBay Rejects GameStop’s $55 Billion Takeover Bid
summary: The online marketplace called the cash-and-stock proposal “neither credible nor attractive.”

title: China Sought Access to Anthropic’s Newest A.I. The Answer Was No.
summary: The latest artificial intelligence models from Anthropic and OpenAI are extending the United States’ lead over China and intensifying the rivalry between the countries.

title: Start-Up Raises $1.3 Billion for an A.I. ‘Grid’
summary: As tech giants dominate the hardware needed to build artificial intelligence, Amp hopes to create an alternative.

title: Wrongful Death Lawsuits Against OpenAI Test a New Strategy
summary: The cases seek to use consumer product safety laws to rein in chatbot companies.

title: Instructure Strikes Deal for Hackers for Return of Canvas Data
summary: Instructure, which provides Canvas software to thousands of schools and universities around the world, did not say what it had given the hackers in exchange for the stolen data.

title: Microsoft’s C.E.O. Intervened When OpenAI Fired Sam Altman, Musk’s Lawyer Claims
summary: Elon Musk’s lawyer argued that Microsoft’s Satya Nadella played a role in getting Mr. Altman his job back at OpenAI when he was briefly fired in 2023.

title: Inside the Elon Musk-OpenAI Trial Courtroom
summary: The tech leaders, with combined net worths exceeding $670 billion, have brought props to court and traded icy stares as their legal dispute reaches a denouement.

title: Google Says Criminal Hackers Used A.I. to Find a Major Software Flaw
summary: The company said that it had identified, for the first time, hackers using artificial intelligence to discover an unknown bug. The attempted attack represents “a taste of what’s to come,” one expert s

title: Meta’s Embrace of A.I. Is Making Its Employees Miserable
summary: As it adapts to the artificial intelligence era, the company is pushing many of its 78,000 workers to use the technology, and preparing to lay some of them off.

title: Can the U.S. Rein in Prediction Markets? + Joanna Stern on Her Year of A.I. Experiments + Our Producer Goes to Attention School
summary: “It seems every other day I am reading a story about a massive insider trading scandal.”

title: The Hopelessness of ‘The Audacity’
summary: In AMC’s merciless satire, the tech lords are extracting all they can from the business (and us) before everything goes to hell.

title: Five Ways A.I. Search Beats an Old-School Google Search
summary: Google’s A.I. search technology is far from perfect (don’t count on it for celebrity news), but it excels at tasks like picking out groceries and detecting scams.

title: Anthropic’s C.E.O. Says It Could Grow by 80 Times This Year
summary: The chief executive, Dario Amodei, said the rapid growth had exponentially increased the start-up’s need for more computing power.

title: Elon Musk’s Confidante Shivon Zilis Is Cast as His Inside Source at OpenAI
summary: Shivon Zilis worked closely with Elon Musk while she was on OpenAI’s board. Her ties to the world’s richest man were detailed in a landmark trial on Wednesday.

title: Meditating or Rebooting? A Robot Buddhist Monk Comes to South Korea.
summary: Gabi, the newest monk at a temple in Seoul that is looking to spread Buddhism, vowed to not overcharge as it seeks enlightenment.
